title: Searching for Submersibles

parameters.purpose: deterministic rescue-planning example for the official PDF-only problem; not observed incident data
parameters.deployment_area: Ionian Sea tourist wreck-search site near a host ship

location_model.model: current-driven dead-reckoning ellipse with two vertical states: seafloor-resting and neutral-buoyancy drifting

scenario_assumptions.purpose: deterministic rescue-planning example for the official PDF-only problem; not observed incident data
scenario_assumptions.deployment_area: Ionian Sea tourist wreck-search site near a host ship

dominant_uncertainties: unknown vertical state: seafloor versus neutral buoyancy
seafloor terrain trapping and density layers

telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.equipment: coded acoustic modem and emergency pinger
telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.uncertainty_reduced: horizontal range and bearing from host ship

telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.equipment: pressure sensor with logged acoustic burst
telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.uncertainty_reduced: distinguishes seafloor contact from neutral-buoyancy drift

telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.equipment: IMU, compass, Doppler velocity log where available

telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.signal: battery, ballast, and propulsion health flags
telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.equipment: fault monitor tied to acoustic status messages
telemetry_to_reduce_uncertainty.uncertainty_reduced: likely mobility, ascent ability, and rescue urgency

equipment_recommendations.model: multi-criteria equipment scoring over coverage, detection quality, readiness, usage cost, and maintenance burden

host_ship_equipment.role: quickly interrogate emergency pingers and reduce horizontal uncertainty

host_ship_equipment.availability: rescue vessel or prearranged regional contractor
host_ship_equipment.maintenance: high: mission planning, batteries, recovery crew
host_ship_equipment.role: expand search when the first host-ship pattern fails
create a local acoustic baseline around the predicted ellipse

host_ship_equipment.availability: host ship or nearby contracted survey vessel
host_ship_equipment.maintenance: medium: tow cable, fish, and winch inspection
host_ship_equipment.role: scan seafloor-likely sectors when the submersible has lost propulsion

rescue_vessel_equipment.reason: needed for confirmed deep contact, intervention, and connection to lifting or life-support systems
keeps the rescue platform over the contact while operating tethered tools

rescue_vessel_equipment.asset: medical decompression and survivor reception team
rescue_vessel_equipment.reason: turns a location success into safe recovery and post-rescue care

equipment_recommendations.recommendation: The host ship should carry acoustic localization, drop beacons, a side-scan option, and an inspection ROV; heavier lift and intervention assets can be pre-contracted for rescue-vessel arrival.

search_plan.model: Bayesian-style survival update with deterministic swept-area coverage over the official location-model uncertainty ellipse

search_probability.detection_realism_note: discounts nominal sensor sweep area for deep-water 3D ambiguity, acoustic multipath, terrain shadowing, and contact-confirmation delay

deployment_points.purpose: deploy USBL and first drop beacon
start creeping-line sonar along drift axis
complete LBL bracket and close acoustic geometry

search_pattern: 0-2 h: interrogate acoustic pingers from the host ship and deploy two LBL beacons around P0/P1
2-8 h: run creeping-line side-scan sonar along the drift-axis ellipse from P1 to P2
8-16 h: use ROV to inspect acoustic/sonar contacts and seafloor terrain traps
16-24 h: add AUV expanding-square search around remaining posterior mass if no confirmed contact

caribbean_adjustments.terrain_adjustment: replace Ionian seafloor-wreck bathymetry with reef wall, trench, and strong surface-current layers
caribbean_adjustments.communications_adjustment: predefine tourist-route LBL boxes because warm shallow shelves may create more multipath and recreational vessel noise

multi_submersible.coordination_rule: assign each submersible a unique acoustic code, maintain non-overlapping safety corridors, and partition the search posterior into Voronoi sectors around last confirmed fixes
multi_submersible.model_change: state vector becomes one ellipse per submersible plus a collision/communication interference constraint matrix
multi_submersible.search_allocation: prioritize sectors by expected lives saved per hour: posterior probability mass divided by deployment time and resource conflicts

government_memo: Memo to the Greek government: MCMS approval should require an auditable lost-submersible plan with periodic acoustic telemetry, a host-ship localization kit, preplanned deployment points, a probability-updated search log, and a rescue-vessel mobilization trigger when the 12-hour posterior still leaves substantial uncertainty.

equipment_rows.role: quickly interrogate emergency pingers and reduce horizontal uncertainty

equipment_rows.availability: rescue vessel or prearranged regional contractor
equipment_rows.maintenance: high: mission planning, batteries, recovery crew
equipment_rows.role: expand search when the first host-ship pattern fails
create a local acoustic baseline around the predicted ellipse
